hero.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 22, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The crowd is pricing a permanent US-Iran peace deal by April 2026 at roughly 1-in-4 odds, a level that assumes either diplomatic miracles or geopolitical shifts the current trajectory doesn't support. The +13 point spike suggests fresh optimism (probably Middle East ceasefire chatter), but 14 months is not enough time to unwind 45 years of sanctions, nuclear disputes, and proxy wars, making this a sucker's rally for bag holders.

daily_take.headline: Iran Peace Deal Odds Jump 13 Points. Smart Money Is Pricing In Hope, Not Reality.
daily_take.deck: US-Iran permanent peace odds spiked to 23.5% on recent diplomatic chatter, a 13-point swing that smells like retail FOMO more than genuine deal momentum. The math doesn't work. 14 months to negotiate a permanent accord when the last serious talks went nowhere over 8 years. Short the rally at 23.5%. Fade the hopium.
